title: Les projets — Plans & the future
objective: Talk about future plans and intentions (A2: can describe plans, hopes and intentions).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: « going to » vs « will »
bridge.body: Two futures: **futur proche** = aller + infinitive (a near, planned “going to”): *Je vais étudier.* And **futur simple** = one word for “will”, formed from the infinitive + **-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont**: *J'étudierai.* Hindi marks the future with verb endings (पढ़ूँगा), so the futur simple’s single-word form will feel natural.

example.french: À l'avenir, je veux travailler en France.
example.englishGloss: In the future, I want to work in France.

example.french: Mon projet est d'étudier à l'étranger.
example.englishGloss: My plan is to study abroad.
Next year, I will study in Paris.

example.french: Je vais économiser pour le voyage.
example.englishGloss: I'm going to save for the trip.

example.french: Beaucoup d'étudiants indiens partent à l'étranger.

example.englishGloss: Next year, I'm going to travel.

exercises.prompt: Futur simple: L'année prochaine, j'___ à Paris. (I will study)

exercises.text: Futur proche: « Demain, je {{}} voyager à Goa. » (aller)

exercises.prompt: « Je vais étudier ce soir » expresses…
exercises.explanation: futur proche (aller + infinitive) = a near-future plan.

exercises.prompt: Build: “Next year, I will work in Mumbai.”
